articles.title: TINDER LOOKS TO AI TO HELP FIGHT ‘SWIPE FATIGUE' AND DATING APP BURNOUT
articles.summary: Tinder is testing a new AI-powered feature called Chemistry to address swipe fatigue and dating app burnout by learning users' interests through questions and optional access to their Camera Roll. The feature aims to replace endless swiping with a smaller set of more relevant matches as Tinder faces declining sign-ups, lower engagement, and subscriber losses. Chemistry is part of a broader strategy that includes AI-driven recommendations, enhanced safety tools, and increased marketing spend to improve relevance, trust, and appeal among Gen Z users.

articles.title: LEAKER SAYS IPHONE 18 PRO KEEPS IPHONE 17 DESIGN
articles.summary: Apple is expected to keep the iPhone 17 Pro design for the iPhone 18 Pro, focusing less on a visual refresh and more on performance after strong market reception. The biggest upgrades are likely the A20/A20 Pro 2nm chips for better speed, efficiency, and on-device AI, with possible front-facing tweaks like a smaller cutout or under-display Face ID.

articles.title: FIGMA ACHIEVES C5 ACCREDITATION
articles.summary: Figma has achieved C5 accreditation from Germany's Federal Office for Information Security, strengthening cloud security and compliance for customers in Germany, Austria, and Switzerland. The accreditation confirms Figma meets rigorous standards for information security, risk management, and operational transparency, making it easier for organizations in the government and finance sectors to use the platform. This milestone builds on Figma's investment in the DACH region, where nearly 90% of DAX 40 companies already use the design collaboration platform.

articles.title: DESIGN SYSTEMS TEAM STRUCTURE IN 2026
articles.summary: Design systems team structures have changed dramatically due to AI capabilities, with tasks that previously required three people in 2023 now often manageable by a single person with the right AI toolkit. Teams are struggling not with headcount but with leverage, as AI can now handle non-judgment work like documentation, auditing, and scaffolding while humans focus on strategic decisions and relationship building. The key is implementing systems that create scale before adding headcount, as teams that understand what to automate versus what to own can outperform much larger teams stuck in inefficient workflows.

articles.title: A DESIGN CONVERSATION AT THE APPLE CREATOR EXPERIENCE
articles.summary: A designer shared his experience at Apple's LA Creator Experience, discussing how tools like Pixelmator Pro and iPad help overcome the fear of starting creative projects. After losing all his work in a 2006 robbery, he started the blog Abduzeedo to document his process and has been using Pixelmator since 2007. He emphasized that good design tools matter because they help creators push through initial creative barriers and start new projects.

articles.title: AI RUNS ON TEXT. SO SHOULD YOU
articles.summary: AI tools like Claude Code are powerful not because they're flashier, but because they work directly with your existing files, making plain text the shared language where human thinking and AI collaboration really scale. The quiet advantage is externalizing your thinking in accessible text, letting AI synthesize, reorganize, and build on it over time without complex systems.

articles.title: COMPARE AND PICK FONTS
articles.summary: Font Mojo is a tool for comparing and selecting fonts from different sources. It allows users to browse fonts with sorting and preview options.

articles.title: PROFESSIONAL COLOR SYSTEM BUILDER
articles.summary: Create accessible palettes with perceptual accuracy, export to 10+ formats, including Tailwind & Figma.

articles.title: ANIMATE SVGS
articles.summary: Stroke lets you create hand-drawn SVG animations for logos, signatures, and illustrations.

articles.title: USING DESIGN TOKENS FOR A PROPORTIONAL TYPOGRAPHIC SCALE
articles.summary: A proportional typographic scale creates a font size hierarchy using consistent mathematical ratios, starting with a base size and multiplying by a fixed ratio to generate larger and smaller sizes. Design tokens are ideal for creating these scales because they can reference other tokens' values and contain mathematical calculations, making it easy to build sophisticated typographic systems with few tokens. This approach ensures visual harmony and consistency across designs while making the system scalable and maintainable for both design and development teams.

articles.title: DESIGNING WITH DATA: A UX RESEARCH GUIDE FOR DESIGNERS
articles.summary: UX research involves understanding user needs through methods such as interviews, surveys, personas, and user testing to inform data-driven design decisions. Key research methods include surveys for quantitative data from many users, one-on-one interviews for deep insights, observation of users in natural environments, and diary studies to track experiences over time. Effective user interviews require thorough preparation, relevant questions, and a focused yet flexible approach to uncover insights that other methods might miss.

articles.title: D8 UNVEILS NEW VISUAL IDENTITY FOR THE CARLTON COLLECTION
articles.summary: D8 Studio created a new visual identity for Carlton Collection to unify 11 independent hotels without erasing their individuality. Guided by the principle “don't duplicate, complement,” the year-long project delivers a cohesive wordmark, flexible typography, and a blue–orange system that ties the portfolio together while celebrating each destination's unique character.

articles.title: DO FOLDABLE PHONES REPRESENT CHALLENGES TO UX APP DESIGN?
articles.summary: Foldable phones pose significant UX challenges due to their variable screen sizes and aspect ratios.

articles.title: 99DESIGNS STUDIES OVER 700 BOOK COVERS FROM THE NEW YORK TIMES
articles.summary: 99designs conducted a study analyzing book cover designs of New York Times bestsellers that examined design trends and patterns among successful book covers.
